name: the-agony-and-the-ecstasy
description: Irving Stone's The Agony and the Ecstasy — a biographical novel of Michelangelo, capturing the creative struggle, discipline, and sacrifice behind the Sistine Chapel ceiling and other masterpieces.
useCase: Understanding the creative struggle, persevering against odds, managing clients while staying true to vision, building discipline, sacrificing for your calling, leaving a legacy.
knownRisks: This is a biographical novel, not a strict biography — some scenes are invented
Michelangelo's life as depicted is extreme — do not take every detail as a model
The book romanticizes suffering — balance with self-care
